scope: security/system-design revocation-lag and authz-drift cohort frontmatter and retrieval-readiness audit
learner_facing_quality_improvement: This wave isolates the non-Pilot security freshness path behind queries like '권한 회수했는데 왜 아직 접근돼요' and 'SCIM disable 뒤 access tail이 남아요', so the next v3 metadata migration can improve first-hit routing in an underweight security/system-design cluster without touching the Pilot baseline.

verification.cohort_selection_rule: curated non-Pilot revocation-lag/authz-drift cohort: authority-transfer-vs-revoke-lag, revocation-propagation-lag, revocation-preview-drift, scim-drift, permission-model-drift, revocation-bus-regional-lag
verification.lint_probe_result: all six sampled docs pass the current authoring lint because they still start with an H1; there is no Pilot conflict and no frontmatter/H1 collision yet in this cohort
verification.retrieval_probe_result: permission-model-drift-authz-graph-design already appears in r3_v20 cohort eval and revocation-bus-regional-lag-recovery-design already appears in the sparse-effect archive, which means this security freshness route is retrieval-active even before v3 metadata is attached

priority_non_pilot_targets.target_query_shape: 권한 이전이랑 revoke lag 뭐가 달라요, disable했는데 still access, authority transfer vs revoke lag basics
priority_non_pilot_targets.why_now: It is the only beginner entrypoint in the cohort, so migrating it first would give repeated learner symptom phrasing a canonical front door in a security category that is still below the intermediate floor.

priority_non_pilot_targets.target_query_shape: logout still works, revoke propagation lag debugging, last accepted after revoke 왜 봐요
priority_non_pilot_targets.why_now: It is the direct incident deep dive for session-tail questions and already sits on a clean H1-only file, making it a safe second-step migration target once frontmatter admission is reopened.

priority_non_pilot_targets.target_query_shape: multi-region revoke lag recovery, stale allow regional tail, revocation bus backlog recovery
priority_non_pilot_targets.why_now: It is the only system-design bridge in the cohort and already shows retrieval activity, so migrating it would connect security incident questions to the underrepresented system-design follow-up path.

guardrails: No Pilot-locked corpus file was modified.
No out-of-scope script change was attempted in this lane.
No corpus doc was edited because this QA wave only recorded a lint-clean non-Pilot migration cohort.
